meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 7 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: How many valence electrons does a neutral phosphorus (P) atom have?
questions.explanation: phosphorus (P) is in a main group whose number of valence electrons is 5.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of N in NH3?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (14/17) x 100 = 82.4%.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the pH of a solution with [H+] = 1 x 10^-7 M?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What ionic charge do elements in group 15 (nitrogen group) most commonly form?
questions.explanation: Elements in group 15 (nitrogen group) typically form ions with a charge of -3.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The bond in HBr has an electronegativity difference of about 0.7. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 0.7 indicates a polar covalent bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, decreasing the pressure by increasing volume of a gaseous a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

choices.label: B
choices.text: causes no shift in the position of equilibrium.

choices.label: C
choices.text: shifts toward the side with more moles of gas.

choices.label: D
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: decreasing the pressure by increasing volume of a gaseous a system at equilibrium shifts toward the side with more moles of gas.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.explanation: The atomic number equals the number of protons; sodium has 11.

questions.stem: Overall, the molecule N2 is best described as
questions.explanation: N2 is a nonpolar molecule because its symmetric shape makes the bond dipoles cancel, giving no net dipole.

questions.stem: Convert 6 cm to millimeters.
questions.explanation: Apply the SI prefix conversion: 6 cm = 60 mm.

questions.stem: Using PV = nRT (R = 0.0821 L·atm/(mol·K)), what is the pressure of 1 mol of gas in 24.6 L at 300 K?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Adding a catalyst to a reaction at equilibrium

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: B
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

choices.label: D
choices.text: speeds up forward and reverse rates equally, leaving the equilibrium position unchanged.

questions.explanation: Catalysts help reach equilibrium faster but do not change K or its position.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 4 m/s through a pipe of cross-section 8 cm^2. What is its speed where the cross-section narrows to 1 cm^2?
questions.explanation: Continuity: A1 v1 = A2 v2, so v2 = (8)(4)/1 = 32 m/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the ground-state electron configuration of a neutral sodium (Z=11) atom?
questions.explanation: Filling orbitals by the Aufbau principle gives sodium: 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s1.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Starting at 6 m/s, an object accelerates at 9.8 m/s^2 over a distance of 8 m. What is its final speed?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A current of 2 A flows for 5 s. How much charge passes a point?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the energy of a photon of frequency 4 x 10^14 Hz? (h = 6.63 x 10^-34 J·s)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure HCl?
questions.explanation: The strongest intermolecular force in HCl is dipole-dipole forces.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The limiting reactant in a reaction is the one that

choices.label: A
choices.text: never appears in the balanced equation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: is completely consumed first and determines the amount of product.

choices.label: C
choices.text: is left over when the reaction stops.

choices.label: D
choices.text: is present in the greatest amount.

questions.explanation: The limiting reactant runs out first, capping the yield; the other is in excess.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas at 2 atm occupies 10 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 1 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (2)(10)/1 = 20 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much heat is needed to raise 250 g of water by 5 degrees C? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 0.5 m/s through a pipe of cross-sectional area 10 m^2. What is the volume flow rate?
questions.explanation: Flow rate Q = A v = (10)(0.5) = 5 m^3/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the larger atomic radius, C or F?

choices.label: C
choices.text: atomic radius shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: C has the larger atomic radius: same period; radius decreases left to right, so C is larger.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object is dropped from rest. Ignoring air resistance, what is its speed after 5 s? (g = 9.8 m/s^2)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Resistors of 10 Ω, 10 Ω, 10 Ω are connected in series. What is the total resistance?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the energy of a photon of wavelength 3 x 10^-7 m? (h = 6.63 x 10^-34 J·s, c = 3.0 x 10^8 m/s)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A double bond consists of

choices.label: B
choices.text: one sigma bond and two pi bonds.

choices.label: C
choices.text: one sigma bond and one pi bond.

questions.explanation: A double bond is 1 sigma + 1 pi; a triple bond is 1 sigma + 2 pi.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of H2SO4? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in H2SO4 gives a molar mass of about 98 g/mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What volume does 5 mol of an ideal gas occupy at STP? (molar volume = 22.4 L/mol)
questions.explanation: V = moles x 22.4 L/mol = 5 x 22.4 = 112 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Adding 4180 J of heat to 200 g of water raises its temperature by how much? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Archimedes' principle states that the buoyant force on a submerged object equals

choices.label: A
choices.text: the weight of the fluid it displaces.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the volume of the object times gravity.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the density of the object times its volume.

questions.explanation: Buoyant force equals the weight of displaced fluid.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Down a group, first ionization energy generally

choices.label: C
choices.text: is always largest at the bottom.

questions.explanation: Outer electrons are farther and more shielded down a group, lowering ionization energy.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object is dropped from rest from a height of 80 m. How long does it take to reach the ground? (g = 9.8 m/s^2)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Two resistors, 60 Ω and 24 Ω, are connected in parallel. What is the equivalent resistance?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Light travels through a medium of refractive index 2.42. What is its speed in that medium? (c = 3.0 x 10^8 m/s)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of SiH4?
questions.explanation: SiH4 adopts a tetrahedral geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many moles are in 20 g of NaOH? (molar mass 40 g/mol)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the conjugate base of the acid HNO3?
questions.explanation: Removing a proton from HNO3 gives its conjugate base, the nitrate ion NO3-.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much heat is required to boil (vaporize) 40 g of water? (latent heat = 2260 J/g)
questions.explanation: q = m L = (40)(2260) = 90400 J for the phase change (no temperature change).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A system absorbs 600 J of heat and does 300 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 600 - 300 = 300 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many neutrons are in an atom of calcium-40? (atomic number 20)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What net force is required to accelerate a 3 kg object at 0.5 m/s^2?
questions.explanation: Newton's second law: F = m a = (3)(0.5) = 1.5 N.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A 0.5 μF capacitor is charged to 24 V. How much charge does it store?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object is 15 cm from a thin converging lens of focal length 10 cm. Where is the image formed?

questions.stem: What is the approximate bond angle in SiH4 (tetrahedral)?
questions.explanation: A tetrahedral molecule such as SiH4 has bond angles of 109.5 degrees.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the mass of 0.25 mol of NaCl? (molar mass 58.5 g/mol)
questions.explanation: mass = moles x molar mass = 0.25 x 58.5 = 14.6 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the molarity of a solution with 1 mol of solute in 3 L of solution?

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = 150 kJ and ΔH2 = -300 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = 150 + -300 = -150 kJ.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas expands by 0.01 m^3 against a constant pressure of 50000 Pa. How much work does it do?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many protons are in an atom of calcium-40?
questions.explanation: The number of protons equals the atomic number; for calcium-40 that is 20.

questions.stem: A net force of 50 N acts on a 6 kg object. What is its acceleration?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Two parallel plates 0.03 m apart have a potential difference of 5 V. What is the electric field between them?
questions.explanation: For parallel plates, E = V / d = 5 / 0.03 = 167 V/m.

questions.stem: For a lens, the object distance is 30 cm and the image distance is 15 cm. What is the magnification?

questions.stem: What is the hybridization of the central atom in CCl4?
questions.explanation: The electron-domain geometry of CCl4 corresponds to sp3 hybridization of the central atom.

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 4 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: molecules = moles x N_A = 4 x 6.022 x 10^23 = 2.41 x 10^24.

questions.stem: If 0.05 L of 3 M solution is diluted to 2 L, what is the new concentration?

questions.stem: A heat engine absorbs 500 J and exhausts 400 J. What is its efficiency?

questions.stem: What is the mass number of calcium-40?
questions.explanation: The mass number counts protons plus neutrons; for calcium-40 it is 40.

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: After 6 half-lives, what fraction of a radioactive sample remains?
questions.explanation: Each half-life halves the amount: fraction = (1/2)^6 = 0.0156.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many valence electrons does a neutral sulfur (S) atom have?
questions.explanation: sulfur (S) is in a main group whose number of valence electrons is 6.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For the reaction CH4 + 2 O2 → CO2 + 2 H2O, how many moles of H2O form from 3 mol of CH4 (with the other reactant in excess)?
questions.explanation: Use the mole ratio 2 H2O : 1 CH4: 3 x 2/1 = 6 mol H2O.

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, adding an inert gas at constant volume to a a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts toward the side with fewer moles of gas.

choices.label: B
choices.text: causes no shift in the position of equilibrium.

choices.label: C
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: D
choices.text: shifts toward the side with more moles of gas.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: adding an inert gas at constant volume to a a system at equilibrium causes no shift in the position of equilibrium.

questions.stem: A sample starts with 40 g of a radioisotope whose half-life is 2 days. How much remains after 6 days?

questions.stem: Hess's law allows the enthalpy of a reaction to be found by

choices.label: B
choices.text: summing the enthalpy changes of steps that add up to the overall reaction.

choices.label: C
choices.text: multiplying the enthalpies of each step.

questions.explanation: Because enthalpy is a state function, step values add to give the overall ΔH.
